title: S2 Works problem
wikitext:
[]: S2 Works problem             Quote Post by Jayfive » Fri Sep 21, 2012 9:25 am Ordered the S2 Works 7CD Box Set from ebay, what I got today was the Taiwanese quasi-bootleg version on K-O Records/K-O Trading. http://k-ocd.com/product.php Yeah, because that looks like the website of company licensed to distribute CDs of every major anime company. So - Not happy. Questions: 1) How unofficial is it? Some wacky licensing deal or a straight-up counterfeit? 2) Does it have 6 or 7 discs? (Its sealed so I cant check) Seller has multiple detailed neg feedbacks saying about bootlegs on: http://www.discogs.com/label/KO+Records http://www.discogs.com/label/SonMay+Records http://www.discogs.com/label/Ever+Anime+International+Records but appears to be insisting that they are legit items. Whether he is in denial or simply hedging his bets by calling the buyers liars im not sure.

[]: Re: S2 Works problem             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Fri Sep 21, 2012 9:33 am Jayfive wrote: 1) How unofficial is it? Some wacky licensing deal or a straight-up counterfeit?

[]:             Quote Post by Jayfive » Fri Sep 21, 2012 9:46 am Shit. Well thanks for the 100% clarification anyway. Oh and theres definately only 6 discs, theres a reference to same on the side of the box. Well this seller is going to get both barrels then. Ive already contacted 2 other sellers who have left positive feedback for buying the same item to compare notes. If he dicks me about, its straight to a dispute, sending the item back tracking postage (which he will pay for) and a full refund. Seller is claiming elsewhere in his feedback that his distibutor told him the items were legit. So either he's full of BS or he's been ripped off himself, either way he's got a buttload of anime CDs which I suspect are all boots. The negs/neuts hes recieved are people who have not contacted him first and hes denied everything, I suspect I will be refunded to keep me sweet and all. But whatever happens the fact he is wilfully selling bootlegs as legit product meaning he gets a neg regardless. And I call eBay CS. Am I cool to link or name the seller on here btw?

[]:             Quote Post by Merkaba » Fri Sep 21, 2012 10:57 am ^ Please do. I recently purchased an Eva CD from eBay that I suspect is a bootleg, and from every single thing you've said, it sound like we ordered from the same person. (The reviews, responses, etc.) HobbyLot. Sound familiar? Or are there other dirtbag bootleg peddlers out there?

[]:             Quote Post by Jayfive » Fri Sep 21, 2012 11:10 am Merkaba wrote: HobbyLot.

[]:             Quote Post by Merkaba » Fri Sep 21, 2012 11:16 am I'm so glad I came across someone else with this same problem (you know what I mean. I'm not happy you got a bootleg, too, but I'm glad I have someone to talk about this with). I've been stressing for the past two days about how I should handle this. (I just ordered the item last week.) I checked last night all of the reviews, after I suspected it was a bootleg, and 40 in the past month have called him out on selling bootlegs. How has eBay not done something already? Has he refunded you yet? Once he refunds you, can he take that money back? I'm scared that he'll refund me, I'll still complain and then he'll take his money back. Also, what can we do to ensure he's taken off of eBay? Did you see how many items he sells? It's RIDICULOUS. I fear that eBay won't do anything because he makes them a TON of money, with how much he sells. We should plot together and make this happen. I've never been ripped off like that before.

[]:             Quote Post by Jayfive » Fri Sep 21, 2012 11:24 am Havent heard back from him yet. He gets one chance. Im not entering into a long discussion over this - I offered him the chance to come up with a resolution, if its not good enough I go to a dispute. Simple as that. I'm scared that he'll refund me, I'll still complain and then he'll take his money back.

[]:             Quote Post by Merkaba » Fri Sep 21, 2012 11:35 am Very good. Thanks for the info. I normally only buy from Amazon, but when I checked eBay and saw the price of the Eva Symphonic album, I had to buy it. Of course, as with anything that's too good to be true, it was, well, too good to be true. I definitely won't be using eBay again, as in my first purchase from it, I was ripped off. I've never had a problem with Amazon. Keep me updated on this thread about how everything goes for you. I'll start my campaign for a refund soon, as well as getting this dirtbag off of eBay. I look forward to seeing how things turn out for you. Good luck.

[]:             Quote Post by Jayfive » Fri Sep 21, 2012 11:50 am Just got off the phone with a saucy-voiced Irish maiden from eBay UK CS. They've put through a report about this guy to eBay US but she also said that from what she could see if that he's already on their radar. She also said for you to ring up ebay where you are - the more complaints that come in, the fastest he gets dealt with. Also, if his account is suspended before he sorts a refund then you can open a dispute and the money will be given back right away. Though I would go through this with ebay CS first, I know I will. Bottom line is whatever happened he won't get to keep your money.

[]:             Quote Post by AR-99 » Fri Sep 21, 2012 12:08 pm In my experience anime CDs from Taiwanese labels tend to be problematic if one is looking for a legit release. The ones that I am familiar with are Sonmay Records (SM) and Everanime but obviously more labels have popped up since. The tipoff tends to be the price since the Japanese releases are generally priced higher, usually much more so. If one is talking economics and doesn't care about the legitimacy, it's very hard to justify paying $45 for what can be had for $15. S2 Works is also an older release so the probability of finding a new, legit version from more than 10 years ago is low. Speaking for myself I generally stay away from buying anime music unless I can get it for a deal (stopped buying SM versions a while ago). The internet has pretty much wiped out my motivation to purchase nearly any form of music in physical form, but to each his own. @Merkaba: Sorry to hear that you had a bad first experience with eBay. There the concept buyer beware applies more so than at a website like Amazon. That being said, I recall of only having made 1 bootleg purchase (1st Appleseed movie DVD) and even then I strongly suspected that it was fake since I got it so close to after the movie release date in the US. Other than that every other Eva goodie that I've picked up from eBay going back more than a decade has been legit. Of course you may need to research what the seller is listing before you pull the trigger and buy if you have doubts, but eBay can be a very good (and potentially addictive) source for Eva goods. I would thus suggest that you not totally blacklist the site because of one bad egg seller. I have to admit though that Hobbylot’s feedback would suggest otherwise since it’s at nearly 12,000 with a 99.7% positive rate. Either other buyers are complicit and happy purchasing bootleg CDs or are ignorant of their actual legitimacy. Regardless, I wish the both of you luck in resolving this issue.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Fri Sep 21, 2012 12:22 pm AR-99 wrote: If one is talking economics and doesn't care about the legitimacy, it's very hard to justify paying $45 for what can be had for $15.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Fri Sep 21, 2012 12:38 pm Hyper Shinchan wrote: I never exactly understood this reasoning, if I can't have a legit release of something, either because it's rare and/or excessively expensive, then I can just settle with an illegal digital copy, either way it's illicit and you're not giving a cent to the Japanese companies that produced it in the first place.

[]:             Quote Post by Jayfive » Fri Sep 21, 2012 12:42 pm AR-99 wrote: S2 Works is also an older release so the probability of finding a new, legit version from more than 10 years ago is low.

[]:             Quote Post by AR-99 » Fri Sep 21, 2012 1:18 pm At the time that I was buying bootleg CDs (10 years ago) it wasn't as easy to get good quality MP3s or full copies of albums like it is today without having to resort to the P2P services like Napster and Kazaa. It did take a bit for me to catch on and learn what was really going on with SM and their cheaper prices. Was just browsing on Rakuten and there is used copy of S2 Works for $23.26 via the Suruyaga storefront. As to the question of addressing bootlegs on the wiki, other examples of merchandise prone to bootlegging (e.g. wall scrolls and laminated posters) could be covered.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Fri Sep 21, 2012 1:24 pm Jayfive wrote: Would it be a good idea to cover bootlegs of Eva albums in the wiki so people dont get caught out?

[]:             Quote Post by Jayfive » Fri Sep 21, 2012 4:02 pm I meant this: http://wiki.evageeks.org/S%C2%B2_Works Its vague to say the least. There's not even a catalogue number or pics of anything but the front. " The U.S. Imported version does not contain the 7th Bonus Disc. Its title is based on the S² Engine. " - So theres every chance the same bootleg edition I have could be mistaken for that. Right here is the level of info you should be aiming for: http://www.discogs.com/Shiroh-Sagisu-Neon-Genesis-Evangelion/release/2481611 http://www.discogs.com/Shiroh-Sagisu-Neon-Genesis-Evangelion-II/release/2481839 http://www.discogs.com/Shiro-Sagisu-The-End-Of-Evangelion/release/1756278 http://www.discogs.com/Shiro-Sagisu-Evangelion-Death/release/1756286 Although this is probably over-doing it a little: http://www.discogs.com/Shiro-Sagisu-Neon-Genesis-Evangelion-III/release/1756303 EDIT: And let me tell you, I have just had to change an absolute bollock-load of entries for Miya/K-O/Ever Anime albums on discogs due to muppets believing this 'Legal in Taiwan but bootleg everywhere else' crap and have added them as 1) not labelled as bootlegs and 2) Country of origin as Japan. So the more info we can give people the better I reckon. Another edit: RIGHT. After a bit of research I've worked it out. Apparently up until 2002 Taiwan wasnt part of the World Trade Organisation and therefore wasn't obliged to enforce international copyright. Now obviously that didn't mean the CDs were licensed or legal in Taiwan, it simply meant that no-one within was stopping the bootleggers and no-one without could stop them. However in 2002 Taiwan joined the WTO and so all bets were off anyway. Some of these labels still operate, but the fact they are bootlegs is simply not open to question.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Fri Sep 21, 2012 5:12 pm Jayfive wrote: Its vague to say the least. There's not even a catalogue number or pics of anything but the front.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Fri Sep 21, 2012 5:29 pm Bagheera wrote: What's vague about it?

[]:             Quote Post by Jayfive » Sat Sep 22, 2012 7:09 am Got a message from the seller basically saying 'oh im sorry I didnt know it was fake, my distro says its legit, send it back for a full refund or you can have $25 back My abridged response: 'Bitch please, you know it's fake and I know you know. 100 negs and neuts all complaining about bootlegs say you do. I'm not some dippy kid you can fob off. Have another go at that resolution thing.'

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sat Sep 22, 2012 7:13 am Jayfive wrote: Got a message from the seller basically saying 'oh im sorry I didnt know it was fake, my distro says its legit, send it back for a full refund or you can have $25 back My abridged response: 'Bitch please, you know it's fake and I know you know. 100 negs and neuts all complaining about bootlegs say you do. I'm not some dippy kid you can fob off. Have another go at that resolution thing.'

S2 Works is also an older release so the probability of finding a new, legit version from more than 10 years ago is low.
